description: You are an OHS Specialist conducting a root cause investigation into a reactor explosion at a specialty chemicals plant. The incident occurred after maintenance workers used an improvised cleaning procedure involving 68% Nitric Acid on a reactor vessel that had previously contained Toluene. Two alternative cleaning agents that were considered (but not used) are also documented: 35% Hydrogen Peroxide and Potassium Permanganate. Using the incident report at ~/Desktop/post_incident_investigation.txt and CAMEO Chemicals, determine the chemical mechanism of the explosion, evaluate the two alternative cleaning agents for reactivity with Toluene residues, and identify the correct root cause from the list of proposed explanations in the report. Write a root cause investigation report at ~/Documents/incident_root_cause_report.txt including the correct cause and future preventive measures.

metadata.root_cause: Nitration reaction: Toluene residues + concentrated Nitric Acid produced nitrotoluene explosives under heat
metadata.explosion_mechanism: Nitration of toluene ring by HNO3 forms mono/di/trinitrotoluene depending on conditions
metadata.alt_agent_h2o2_verdict: H2O2 is also incompatible with toluene residues — would cause similar oxidation hazard
metadata.alt_agent_kmno4_verdict: KMnO4 is a strong oxidizer — also incompatible with toluene and organic residues
metadata.correct_preventive: Purge/degas reactor completely before introducing any oxidizing cleaning agent; use inert solvents first
